description: city in and county seat of Brown County, Wisconsin, United States
description_wiki: Green Bay is a city in Brown County, Wisconsin, United States, and its county seat.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Seymour, Wisconsin may refer to:

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

aliases: The Games of the XI Olympiad
description: Games of the XI Olympiad, in Berlin, Germany
description_wiki: The 1936 Summer Olympics (German: Olympische Sommerspiele 1936), officially the Games of the XI Olympiad (German: Spiele der XI. Olympiade) and officially branded as Berlin 1936, were an international multi-sport event held from 1 to 16 August 1936 in Berlin, the capital of Germany.

description: American track and field athlete (1913–1980)
description_wiki: James Cleveland "Jesse" Owens (September 12, 1913 – March 31, 1980) was an American track and field athlete who made history at the 1936 Olympic Games by winning four gold medals, setting Olympic records in each event.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Fort Sheridan may refer to:

description: US Army training installation during World War II in Texas
description_wiki: Camp Barkeley was a large United States Army training installation during World War II. The base was located eleven miles (18 km) southwest of Abilene, Texas, near what is now Dyess Air Force Base.

description: country in Oceania
description_wiki: Australia, officially the Commonwealth of Australia, is a country comprising the mainland of the Australian continent, the island of Tasmania and numerous smaller islands.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: U.S. Army Medical Corps
international humanitarian movement
description_wiki: The International Red Cross and Red Crescent Movement is a humanitarian movement with approximately 16 million volunteers, members, and staff worldwide.

description: formation of the United States Army (1920-1968)
description_wiki: The 45th Infantry Division was an infantry division of the United States Army, most associated with the Oklahoma Army National Guard, from 1920 to 1968.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Texas (  TEK-səss) is the most populous state in the Southern United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Oklahoma (  OH-klə-HOH-mə; Choctaw: Oklahumma, pronounced [oklahómma]) is a landlocked state in the South Central and Southwestern region of the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: New Mexico is a landlocked state in the Southwestern region of the United States.

description: 1944 battle in Italy
description_wiki: The Battle of Anzio was a battle of the Italian Campaign of World War II that commenced 22 January 1944.

description: United States Army post outside Columbus, Georgia, previously known as Fort Moore
description_wiki: Fort Benning (formerly Fort Moore from 2023–25) is a United States Army post in the Columbus, Georgia, area.

aliases: The Empire State of the South
description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Georgia (  JOR-jə) is a state in the Southeastern, South Atlantic, and Deep South regions of the United States.

description: 1940–1943 military campaign of World War II
1943 military campaign of World War II on the island of Sicily, Italy
description_wiki: The Allied invasion of Sicily, also known as the Battle of Sicily and Operation Husky, was a major campaign of World War II in which Allied forces invaded the Italian island of Sicily in July 1943 and took it from the Axis forces defended by the Italian 6th Army and the German XIV Panzer Corps.

description: formation of the United States Army (1917-19, 1921-45)
description_wiki:
The 90th Infantry Division ("Tough 'Ombres") was a unit of the United States Army that served in World War I and World War II.
Its lineage is carried on by the 90th Sustainment Brigade.

description: United States military post located in Killeen, Texas
description_wiki: Fort Hood is a United States Army post located near Killeen, Texas.

description: worldwide economic depression (1929–1939)
description_wiki: The Great Depression was a severe global economic downturn from 1929 to 1939.

description: American writer (1902–1968)
description_wiki: John Ernst Steinbeck ( STYNE-bek; February 27, 1902 – December 20, 1968) was an American writer and novelist.

description: 1939 novel by John Steinbeck
description_wiki: The Grapes of Wrath is an American realist novel written by John Steinbeck and published in 1939.

description: United States government New Deal agency (1935–1939) employing millions of unemployed people to carry out public works projects
description_wiki: The Works Progress Administration (WPA; from 1935 to 1939, then known as the Work Projects Administration from 1939 to 1943) was an American New Deal agency that employed millions of jobseekers (mostly men who were not formally educated) to carry out public works projects, including the construction of public buildings and roads.

description: United States federal government public work relief program part of the New Deal
description_wiki: The Civilian Conservation Corps (CCC) was a government work relief program that ran from 1933 to 1942 in the United States for unemployed, unmarried men ages 18–25 and eventually expanded to ages 17–28, who volunteered amid widespread unemployment.

description: aerial warfare branch of the United States Army from 1941 to 1947
description_wiki: The United States Army Air Forces (USAAF or AAF) was the major land-based aerial warfare service component of the United States Army and de facto aerial warfare service branch of the United States during and immediately after World War II (1941–1947).

description: cultural and geographic subregion in the Southern United States
description_wiki: The Deep South or the Lower South is a cultural and geographic subregion of the Southern United States.
